amendments.normalized_body: In `process_backend.rs` timeout path (~line 1048-1049), `spawn_background_reap` is called to kill the child but the `prepared.preserve_failed_artifacts()` is only called later in the `invoke()` method (~line 1230-1233), not in `spawn_and_wait()`. This means the `spawn_and_wait()` timeout path returns the error *without* cleaning up temp files (schema/message paths for Codex). The cleanup happens at the caller level, which is correct. However, if `spawn_and_wait()` is ever called from a different call site that doesn't handle the Timeout failure_class specially, temp files will leak. Consider moving `prepared.preserve_failed_artifacts()` into a single cleanup path or documenting the cleanup contract more explicitly so future callers don't miss it.

The backoff `backoff_for_attempt(cursor.attempt)` is called with the *current* attempt number (1-indexed, starting at 1) before `cursor.retry()` increments it. This means the very first retry (after attempt 1 fails) waits 5 seconds. For the common case of a transient JSON parse error that would succeed immediately on retry, this adds unnecessary latency. Consider using `backoff_for_attempt(cursor.attempt - 1)` or starting the base delay at attempt 2 so the first retry is immediate and backoff kicks in only on repeated failures.

Preserve invoke-time backend availability classifications in `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:383-408`. `map_invoke_error` currently converts every non-`Invocation*` error into `InvocationFailed { failure_class: TransportFailure }`, which discards the new `BackendUnavailable.failure_class` metadata. That means invoke-time `BackendUnavailable { failure_class: Some(BinaryNotFound) }` from `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:396-401` or `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:789-800` still burns the retry budget instead of failing terminally.

Fix the process-backed OpenRouter command builder in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:802-808` so a missing or blank `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is treated as the same terminal infrastructure failure used elsewhere. This path currently only checks whether `std::env::var` returned `Ok`, so `""` is accepted and a missing key becomes `InvocationFailed { failure_class: TransportFailure }`. That contradicts `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1200-1208` and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:68-77`, and the stale expectation in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:1394-1421` should be updated with it.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: The backoff `backoff_for_attempt(cursor.attempt)` is called with the *current* attempt number (1-indexed, starting at 1) before `cursor.retry()` increments it. This means the very first retry (after attempt 1 fails) waits 5 seconds. For the common case of a transient JSON parse error that would succeed immediately on retry, this adds unnecessary latency. Consider using `backoff_for_attempt(cursor.attempt - 1)` or starting the base delay at attempt 2 so the first retry is immediate and backoff kicks in only on repeated failures.

Preserve invoke-time backend availability classifications in `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:383-408`. `map_invoke_error` currently converts every non-`Invocation*` error into `InvocationFailed { failure_class: TransportFailure }`, which discards the new `BackendUnavailable.failure_class` metadata. That means invoke-time `BackendUnavailable { failure_class: Some(BinaryNotFound) }` from `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:396-401` or `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:789-800` still burns the retry budget instead of failing terminally.

Fix the process-backed OpenRouter command builder in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:802-808` so a missing or blank `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is treated as the same terminal infrastructure failure used elsewhere. This path currently only checks whether `std::env::var` returned `Ok`, so `""` is accepted and a missing key becomes `InvocationFailed { failure_class: TransportFailure }`. That contradicts `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1200-1208` and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:68-77`, and the stale expectation in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:1394-1421` should be updated with it.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 3 amendment(s); restart required.
